questions.q: Which of the following primary activities is mainly responsible for deforestation?
Describe the significance of forestry as a primary activity.
questions.answer: Forestry provides timber, fuelwood, and non-timber forest products; helps in soil conservation and climate regulation; and supports biodiversity.

questions.q: Explain how mining can lead to environmental degradation.
questions.answer: Mining can lead to soil erosion, water pollution, and loss of biodiversity. It also releases harmful gases and toxic substances into the atmosphere, causing air pollution.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Forestry is an important primary activity because it provides a large amount of _______________________ every year.
What is the main difference between subsistence agriculture and commercial agriculture?
questions.answer: Subsistence agriculture is practiced for personal consumption, whereas commercial agriculture is done for profit.

questions.q: True or False: Forestry is a primary activity that contributes to greenhouse gas emissions.
Describe the role of agriculture in the economy of a country.
questions.answer: Agriculture provides employment, food, and raw materials for industries; contributes to GDP; and helps in poverty alleviation.

questions.q: Explain how mining can lead to social problems.
questions.answer: Mining can lead to displacement of people, loss of livelihoods, and social unrest. It also creates health problems due to exposure to toxic substances.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The main advantage of subsistence agriculture is that it provides _______________________ food for the family.
What is the significance of forestry in the context of environmental conservation?
questions.answer: Forestry helps in soil conservation, climate regulation, and biodiversity conservation; and provides ecosystem services.
